clue: Putting old fiddler ahead of America is hard to bear
blog: O (old) + NERO (reference the legend that Emperor Nero  [37 – 68] fiddled while Rome burned) + US ([United States] of America])

clue: Withdrawing food with reduced fat is criminal
blog: GRUB (food) reversed (withdrawing) + LAR d (fat) excluding the final letter (reduced) D

clue: Supplement intermittently reducing stress
blog: TES (letters remaining in s T r E s S when srs, letters 1 , 3 and 5 , [intermittently] are removed )

clue: Take in study defending male convict
blog: CON (trick; deceive; take in) + ( DEN [study] containing [defending] M [male])

clue: The Independent left faltering in the dock
blog: Anagram of (faltering) THE and I (independent) and L (left)

clue: Indict leaders removed from slack school
blog: l IMP (slack) excluding the first letter (leader) L + t EACH (to school) excluding the first letter (leader) T – therefore leaders removed

clue: Sibyl, swapping outer layers for gown, starts to read some tea leaves
blog: si B yl with the outer two letters at either end of the word SI and YL replaced by (swapping … for] ROBE (gown) and RS ( initial letters of [starts to] each of R ead and S ome) – SI swopped for ROB abd YL swapped for ERS

clue: Pat rolled over lazily without enthusiasm
blog: PET (fondle, pat) reversed (rolled over) + IDLY (lazily)

clue: Obsessive swimmer cut around island
blog: GROUPE r (large fish; swimmer) excluding the final letter (cut) R containing (around) I (island)

clue: Serious enjoyment cycling by a French city
blog: F UN (enjoyment) with the letters cycled one place left or round to the end to form UN F + UN (one of the French forms of ‘a’) + NY (New York; city)

clue: Provide with a piece from Carmen
blog: ARM ( hidden word in [piece from c ARM en)

clue: Digs ads showcasing Spielberg movie
Students absorbed by ecstasy trip
blog: ( L [student] + L [student] giving students) contained in (absorbed by) JOY (ecstasy)

clue: Sci-fi character almost upset the Force?
blog: SPOC k (reference Spock, a fictional character in the Star Trek media franchise) excluding the final letter (almost) K and then reversed (upset)

clue: English press picked up prize
blog: E (English) + STEEM ( sounds like [picked up] STEAM [iron; press])

clue: Panorama, say, probing fiscal watchdog is comparatively offensive
blog: SCENE (panorama) contained in (probing) OBR (Office for Budget Responsibility [a fiscal watchdog])

clue: Telegraph's lead editor, admitting awful blunder, is one wheeled out for retirement?
blog: ( T [ first letter of {lead} T elegraph] + ED [editor]) containing (admitting) an anagram of (awful) BLUNDER

clue: One of two wind instruments isn't keeping time. It's exhausting!
blog: FLUt E (a wind instrument) excluding (isn’t keeping) T (time) + PIPE (a second wind instrument)

clue: Pickled hard corncobs originally set aside for wild horses
blog: Anagram of (pickled) H (hard, in pencil lead) and c ORNCOBS excluding (set aside) the first letter [originally] C

clue: Small step for man essentially inspiring a work of science fiction
blog: ( S [small] + PACE [step] + O [ middle letter of [essentially] f O r] + A [ middle letter of [essentially] m A n) containing (inspiring) PER (a)

clue: Briefly land joke in conversation that’s crude
blog: INDI a (country; land) excluding the final letter (briefly) A + GEST ( sounds like [in conversation] JEST [joke])

clue: Opens bin out for unwanted parts of fish
blog: Anagram of (out) OPENS BIN

clue: They come across rash in hospital round linked with irregular deaths
blog: H (hospital) + O (a round character) + an anagram of (irregular) DEATHS

blog: PLUM (a fruit) B at S excluding (scratching) AT

Happy with agent set up to take the place of a traveller
blog: G a Y (carefree and merry; happy) with SPY (agent) reversed (set ip; down entry) to replace (take the place of) A
